author: y4rr
content:
Phase 5 V9: TieredBlockStore<T> generic over BlockElement (DeviceRepr + ValidAsZeroBits + bytemuck::Pod + Copy + Default + 'static). Default T = u16 keeps backward-compat. Disk serialisation via bytemuck::cast_slice (was bf16-specific bf16_bits_to_bytes). Internal block_bytes_t() computes size_of::<T>() * numel. New test: store_works_with_i8_element_for_quant_extensibility — verifies a TieredBlockStore<i8> end-to-end with insert_host + ensure_resident + clone_dtoh round trip.

Full Llama 3 8B stack now ships:
* Paged MLP (Gate + Up + DownT) via TieredBlockStore<u16>, V2 hybrid (hot/cold pinning + SVD predictor + TEAL refinement)
* Paged embed_tokens (PagedEmbedding row-block paging)
* Paged lm_head (PagedLmHead lazy-load)
* KV cache + decode mode
* NVMe L3 tier (Vram → Host → Disk)
* internal tooling
* always-resident floor reduced by ~5.7 GiB vs baseline

81 tests pass, no warnings.

Remaining V3 axes (deliberately deferred):
* Async DMA prefetch — gated on internal toolingr ~400 lines of code) and a head-level sparsity predictor. Largest VRAM win on 70B+ models; on 8B saves only ~2.5 GiB, low ROI without 70B target hardware.
* KV cache paging — full causal attention requires every cached position every step, so spilling is bandwidth-bound. Only useful with sliding-window or sparse attention variants (which are model-architecture changes, not paging changes).
